mission.title: Restore kubeconfig credentials when the console reports MISSING_CREDENTIALS
mission.description: Repair mission for kubeconfig credentials setup. Use it when the console preflight reports MISSING_CREDENTIALS, when kubectl cannot find a kubeconfig, or when the local kubeconfig exists but lacks usable users or contexts.

steps.title: Inspect the current kubeconfig state
steps.description: Check whether a kubeconfig file exists and whether it already contains contexts or users. This confirms whether you are missing the file entirely or just missing credentials inside it.
commands:
test -f "$HOME/.kube/config" && ls -l "$HOME/.kube/config" || echo "No kubeconfig at $HOME/.kube/config"
kubectl config get-contexts 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl config view --raw --minify 2>/dev/null || true

steps.title: Back up any existing kubeconfig
steps.description: Create a timestamped backup before writing new credentials so you can restore the prior state if the imported kubeconfig is wrong.
commands:
mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube"
if test -f "$HOME/.kube/config"; then
  cp "$HOME/.kube/config" "$HOME/.kube/config.backup-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
fi

steps.title: Install kubeconfig from SOURCE_KUBECONFIG or local admin.conf
steps.description: If you already have a kubeconfig file, export SOURCE_KUBECONFIG to its path and copy it into place. On a local control-plane host, the mission falls back to /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf automatically.
commands:
mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube"
if test -n "${SOURCE_KUBECONFIG:-}" && test -f "$SOURCE_KUBECONFIG"; then
  cp "$SOURCE_KUBECONFIG" "$HOME/.kube/config"
elif test -f /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf; then
  sudo cp /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf "$HOME/.kube/config"
  sudo chown "$USER" "$HOME/.kube/config"
else
  echo "Set SOURCE_KUBECONFIG to a valid kubeconfig path or run on a control-plane node with /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf"
  exit 1
fi
chmod 600 "$HOME/.kube/config"

steps.title: Validate contexts and current user
steps.description: After writing the kubeconfig, confirm it now contains contexts and usable credentials.
commands:
kubectl config get-contexts
kubectl config current-context
kubectl auth whoami

steps.title: Confirm cluster reachability with the restored credentials
steps.description: Run a low-risk API read to verify the missing-credentials condition is gone and the console can reuse the same kubeconfig successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Cloud-managed cluster requires provider login
troubleshooting.description: If the kubeconfig uses an exec plugin and still fails, refresh the cloud credentials first and then retry. Common examples include AWS IAM, GKE gcloud auth, and Azure az login based kubeconfigs.

troubleshooting.title: Kubeconfig has contexts but no working users
troubleshooting.description: Open the kubeconfig with kubectl config view --raw and verify the users section contains a token, client certificate, or exec plugin stanza for the selected context.

resolution.summary: A working kubeconfig was restored or installed, which resolves MISSING_CREDENTIALS by re-establishing contexts, users, and API connectivity for kubectl and the console.

prerequisites.resources: A valid kubeconfig file or access to /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf on a control-plane host.
prerequisites.description: Export SOURCE_KUBECONFIG if you already received a kubeconfig file from a cluster administrator or your cloud provider.
